{\\n530:         Map<String, ConsumerGroupPartitionAssignor> defaultAssignors = CONSUMER_GROUP_BUILTIN_ASSIGNORS\\n531:             .stream()\\n532:             .collect(Collectors.toMap(ConsumerGroupPartitionAssignor::name, Function.identity()));\\n533: \\n534:         List<ConsumerGroupPartitionAssignor> assignors = new ArrayList<>();\\n535: \\n536:               if (object instanceof String klass) {\\n541:                     assignor = defaultAssignors.get(klass);\\n542:                     if (assignor == null) {\\n543:                   throw new KafkaException(\\\"Class \\\" + klass + \\\" cannot be found\\\", e);\\n547:                         } catch (ClassCastException e) {\\n548:                             throw new KafkaException(klass + \\\" is not an instance of \\\" + ConsumerGroupPartitionAssignor.class.getName());\\n549:                         }\\n550:                     }\\n551:                 } else if (object instanceof Class<?> klass) {\\n552:                     Object o = Utils.newInstance((Class<?>) klass);\\n553:                     if (!ConsumerGroupPartitionAssignor.class.isInstance(o)) {\\n554:                         throw new KafkaException(klass + \\\" is not an instance of \\\" + ConsumerGroupPartitionAssignor.class.getName());\\n555:                     }\\n556:                     assignor = (ConsumerGroupPartitionAssignor) o;\\n557:                 } else {\\n558:                     throw new KafkaException(\\\"Unexpected element of type \\\" + object.getClass().getName() + \\\", expected String or Class\\\");\\n559:                 }\\n560: \\n561:                 assignors.add(assignor);\\n562: \\n563:                 if (assignor instanceof Configurable configurable) {\\n564:                     configurable.configure(config.originals());\\n565:                 }\\n566:             }\\n567:         } catch (Exception e) {\\n568:             for (ConsumerGroupPartitionAssignor assignor : assignors) {\\n569:                 maybeCloseQuietly(assignor, \\\"AutoCloseable object constructed and configured during failed call to consumerGroupAssignors\\\");\\n570:             }\\n571:             throw e;\\n572:         }\\n573: \\n574:         return assignors;\\n575:     }\\n576: \\n577:     protected List<ShareGroupPartitionAssignor> shareGroupAssignors(\\n578:         AbstractConfig config\\n579:     ) {\\n580: Configuration Classes\\n\\n**ConfigDef and ConfigKey (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java)**:\\nThe `ConfigDef` class is the central configuration definition framework in Kafka. It defines a nested `ConfigKey` static class (lines 1237-1294) that represents a single configuration parameter with properties like name, type, documentation, default value, validator, importance level, group, display name, and dependents. This is NOT a Java annotation but rather a class used to define configuration metadata. The ConfigDef class provides methods like `toHtml()` to generate HTML documentation from configuration definitions, and `getConfigValue()` to extract specific configuration values.\\n\\n**AbstractConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java)**:\\nThis is the base class for all configuration classes in Kafka. It uses a `ConfigDef` instance to define and validate configurations. Key methods include `documentationOf()` and `typeOf()` which retrieve metadata about specific configuration keys from the ConfigDef.\\n\\n## Broker and Core Configuration\\n\\n**AbstractKafkaConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/AbstractKafkaConfig.java)**:\\nA comprehensive AbstractConfig subclass that merges multiple ConfigDef objects from various coordinators and configuration sources. It serves as the unified configuration for Kafka brokers in KRaft mode.\\n\\n**KafkaConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala)**:\\nA Scala-based configuration class containing a `main()` method (lines 59-61) that generates HTML documentation using `configDef.toHtml(4, (config: String) => \\\"brokerconfigs_\\\" + config, DynamicBrokerConfig.dynamicConfigUpdateModes)`. This is one of the primary configuration documentation generators in the build system.\\n\\n**LogConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/LogConfig.java)**:\\nManages topic and log-level configurations. Contains a nested `LogConfigDef` class (lines 94-120) that extends ConfigDef with custom header handling for server defaults. Referenced in build.gradle as the class to generate topic configuration HTML documentation.\\n\\n## Coordinator Configurations\\n\\n**GroupCoordinatorConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 group-coordinator/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupCoordinatorConfig.java)**:\\nDefines configuration for the group coordinator, merging consumer group and share group configurations.\\n\\n**ShareGroupConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 group-coordinator/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/coordinator/group/modern/share/ShareGroupConfig.java)**:\\nConfiguration for share group functionality.\\n\\n## Configuration Documentation Generators\\n\\n**TransformationDoc (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/TransformationDoc.java)**:\\nA documentation generator with a `main()` method (lines 79-81) that uses reflection to discover transformation classes with CONFIG_DEF static fields and generates HTML documentation from their configurations.\\n\\n**PredicateDoc (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/PredicateDoc.java)**:\\nSimilar to TransformationDoc, this generates HTML documentation for predicate configurations by reflecting on their CONFIG_DEF fields.\\n\\n**MirrorHeartbeatConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 connect/mirror/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/mirror/MirrorHeartbeatConfig.java)**:\\nContains a `main()` method (lines 84-86) that directly generates HTML using `defineHeartbeatConfig(new ConfigDef()).toHtml()`, demonstrating the fluent API for configuration documentation generation.\\n\\n## AbstractConfig Subclasses\\n\\nNumerous classes extend AbstractConfig and define static ConfigDef fields:\\n- MirrorClientConfig, MirrorConnectorConfig, MirrorMakerConfig\\n- TaskConfig, WorkerConfig, RestServerConfig\\n- ClientMetricsConfigs, ConverterConfig\\n- FileStreamSourceConnector, FileStreamSinkConnector\\n- Various transformation classes (InsertHeader, ValueToKey, DropHeaders, ExtractField, TimestampRouter)\\n\\n## Configuration Framework\\n\\n**Coordination Configurations**:\\n- TransactionLogConfig, TransactionStateManagerConfig (transaction coordinator)\\n- AddPartitionsToTxnConfig (add partitions to transaction)\\n- ShareCoordinatorConfig (share group coordinator)\\n- MetadataLogConfig (KRaft metadata log)\\n\\n**Server Configurations**:\\n- KRaftConfigs (KRaft mode specific)\\n- MetricConfigs (metrics collection)\\n- DelegationTokenManagerConfigs (delegation tokens)\\n\\nThe pattern demonstrates that Kafka uses a declarative ConfigDef-based approach rather than Java annotations for configuration documentation. Each ConfigDef instance contains ConfigKey objects that fully specify configuration parameters, types, defaults, validators, and documentation. The toHtml() method chain (defined in ConfigDef) converts these definitions into HTML documentation, which is used by build tasks to generate broker configuration documentation HTML files.\"\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10785,"cache_read_input_tokens":106144,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10785},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3d5d61d1-eeda-443a-b230-f3ef40629460","uuid":"f96dded1-d0c4-4d26-8773-1ed059500095"}

configuration definition framework in Kafka. It defines a nested `ConfigKey` static class (lines 1237-1294) that represents a single configuration parameter with properties like name, type, documentation, default value, validator, importance level, group, display name, and dependents. This is NOT a Java annotation but rather a class used to define configuration metadata. The ConfigDef class provides methods like `toHtml()` to generate HTML documentation from configuration definitions, and `getConfigValue()` to extract specific configuration values.\\n\\n**AbstractConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java)**:\\nThis is the base class for all configuration classes in Kafka. It uses a `ConfigDef` instance to define and validate configurations. Key methods include `documentationOf()` and `typeOf()` which retrieve metadata about specific configuration keys from the ConfigDef.\\n\\n## Broker and Core Configuration\\n\\n**AbstractKafkaConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/AbstractKafkaConfig.java)**:\\nA comprehensive AbstractConfig subclass that merges multiple ConfigDef objects from various coordinators and configuration sources. It serves as the unified configuration for Kafka brokers in KRaft mode.\\n\\n**KafkaConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala)**:\\nA Scala-based configuration class containing a `main()` method (lines 59-61) that generates HTML documentation using `configDef.toHtml(4, (config: String) => \\\"brokerconfigs_\\\" + config, DynamicBrokerConfig.dynamicConfigUpdateModes)`. This is one of the primary configuration documentation generators in the build system.\\n\\n**LogConfig (github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489 storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/LogConfig.java)**:\\nManages topic and log-level configurations. AddPartitionsToTxnConfig (add partitions to transaction)\\n- ShareCoordinatorConfig (share group coordinator)\\n- MetadataLogConfig (KRaft metadata log)\\n\\n**Server Configurations**:\\n- KRaftConfigs (KRaft mode specific)\\n- MetricConfigs (metrics collection)\\n- DelegationTokenManagerConfigs (delegation tokens)\\n\\nThe pattern demonstrates that Kafka uses a declarative ConfigDef-based approach rather than Java annotations for configuration documentation. Each ConfigDef instance contains ConfigKey objects that fully specify configuration parameters, types, defaults, validators, and documentation.
